beats.title: Morning at Raven's Hold
beats.boundaryReason: The beat covers the wake-up recap, character check-in, and first sighting of the approaching gnolls before the first attack is launched.
dateEvidence: The opening narration places the scene at morning on 1748-04-13 at Raven's Hold.
The party has only just awakened and begun preparing breakfast when the threat appears.

beats.title: Demon Falls at the Gate
beats.boundaryReason: Combat begins with Wellby's opening shot and stays focused on the initial ranged exchange while the party holds the towers.
dateEvidence: The attack follows immediately from the same morning wake-up scene with no time break.
The session note describes the demon being killed first as the opening turn of the fight.

beats.title: Holding the Gatehouse Door
beats.boundaryReason: The fight shifts into the choke-point defense at the doors, with Kenzo preparing to hold the opening as the gnolls press forward.
dateEvidence: The combat continues continuously from the opening exchange on the same morning.
The action remains at the gatehouse with no travel or rest transition.

beats.title: Gnolls Breach the Gate
beats.boundaryReason: The doors are open and the melee turns into the main gate struggle until only one fleeing gnoll remains.
dateEvidence: This is the same uninterrupted battle sequence on 1748-04-13.
The transcript has not advanced beyond the early morning fight at Raven's Hold.

beats.title: Last Gnoll Breaks Away
beats.boundaryReason: The last gate gnoll flees and the party improvises ropes, movement, and finishing blows before the chase properly begins.
dateEvidence: The fleeing gnoll sequence happens immediately after the gate fight with no pause.
Hyenas are still asleep from the same ongoing combat scene, confirming the same morning.

beats.title: Chasing the Fleeing Gnoll
beats.boundaryReason: The scene moves from the gate to the outer plaza, where Kenzo and Wellby pursue the fleeing gnoll and the party clears the last attackers.
dateEvidence: The pursuit runs directly out of the gate combat with no elapsed-day change.
The transcript still identifies the time as morning during the chase.

beats.title: Exploring the Outer Plaza
beats.boundaryReason: Once the last gnolls die, the party shifts into exploration of the plaza, stables, barracks, and the captured animals.
dateEvidence: The DM states it is about eight in the morning after the fighting ends.
The party moves directly into scouting, talking to animals, and searching the ruined buildings.

beats.title: Theorizing Over Breakfast
beats.boundaryReason: The exploration settles into discussion, theory-building about the demonic corruption, a short rest, and the decision to investigate further next time.
dateEvidence: The party takes a short rest and breakfast after the fighting, with the sun only a bit higher in the sky.
No overnight break or date change occurs before the session ends.
